project.name: Tax Authority Enterprise RAG
project.description: Enterprise RAG system for a national tax authority with zero-hallucination citations, RBAC filtering inside HNSW, hybrid BM25+dense retrieval, AWS Bedrock, and OpenTelemetry observability.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Shared pytest fixtures and dataclasses for the Tax Authority RAG evaluation suite, including the MockRAGClient stub, UserContext/ChunkMeta/RAGResponse models, synthetic corpus, role-based user fixtures, AWS Bedrock clients, Redis/OpenSearch handles, and all promotion-gate threshold constants.

nodes.name: seed_opensearch.py
nodes.summary: Standalone script that creates the 'tax-docs-test' OpenSearch index with Lucene HNSW k-NN settings and bulk-indexes ~250 synthetic Dutch tax-law document chunks (MOCK_CORPUS plus generated fixtures) for integration test seeding.

nodes.name: test_ambiguity_refusal.py
nodes.summary: Tests that out-of-corpus, under-specified, and contradictory queries trigger structured refusals with zero citations and no hallucinated answers, validating the CRAG Irrelevant/Ambiguous verdict path and attempt-count caps.

nodes.name: test_citation_accuracy.py
nodes.summary: Enforces the 1.00 citation-accuracy promotion gate by parsing citation anchors from generated answers, verifying full hierarchy depth (doc_id, article, lid, onderdeel, sub, ECLI), and running Haiku NLI entailment checks against retrieved chunks.

nodes.name: test_hybrid_retrieval.py
nodes.summary: Tests the hybrid BM25 + dense retrieval cascade with deterministic simulators, verifying ECLI exact-match boosting, RRF fusion correctness, nDCG@5 improvement after reranking, and top-K cascade sizing per MASTER-PLAN constants.

nodes.name: test_latency_budgets.py
nodes.summary: Validates p95/p99 TTFT and end-to-end latency SLAs against the Module 4 promotion gates (p95 TTFT <= 1500 ms, p95 E2E <= 4000 ms, retrieval p95 <= 300 ms) using a 100-query smoke run against the mock client.

nodes.name: test_observability.py
nodes.summary: Verifies that every RAG request emits OTel spans with the required attribute set (trace_id, user_role, Bedrock token counts, grader verdict) and enforces Ragas-style ContextPrecision, ContextRecall, and AnswerRelevancy gates.

nodes.name: test_rbac_redteam.py
nodes.summary: Adversarial RBAC red-team suite covering direct FIOD title attacks, semantic proximity attacks, cache poisoning across roles, prompt injection and role impersonation, structured-refusal existence-disclosure prevention, legal-counsel ceiling violations, and redaction guard audit integrity.

nodes.name: test_semantic_cache.py
nodes.summary: Tests that the semantic cache key is role-bound, year-bound, and classification-bound via SHA-256, that the 0.97 cosine threshold correctly blocks year-confusion near-misses, and that Redis round-trip TTL stays within the 24-hour ceiling.

nodes.name: test_temporal_correctness.py
nodes.summary: Validates that retrieval returns the legislatively correct version for a given tax year by enforcing valid_from/valid_to date-range filtering, testing year-confusion near-misses (2021 vs 2024 Box 1 rates), and verifying superseded-document disclosure metadata.

nodes.name: ChunkMeta
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a retrieved document chunk with full citation hierarchy (doc_id, article, lid, onderdeel, sub, ECLI/ELI), classification level, temporal validity fields, and relevance score.

nodes.name: RAGResponse
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing the full output of a RAG pipeline call: answer text, citation list, grader verdict, retrieved chunks, structured refusal payload, latency metrics, cache-hit flag, retry counters, and Bedrock token usage.

nodes.name: UserContext
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding an authenticated user's identity, role (helpdesk/inspector/legal/fiod), and classification ceiling, with a property that resolves the allowed classification levels from the ALLOWED_LEVELS role matrix.

nodes.name: MockRAGClient
nodes.summary: Deterministic RAG pipeline stub that applies real RBAC pre-filtering and temporal year-matching during retrieval, then produces grounded answers with structured citations or structured refusals without any live Bedrock calls.

nodes.name: HaikuJudge
nodes.summary: LLM-as-judge wrapper around Bedrock Claude Haiku 4.5 that provides structured tool-use verdicts for entailment checking, refusal evaluation, and faithfulness scoring used in integration-marked citation and RBAC tests.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Excludes secrets, Python artifacts, raw corpus data, dev tooling, and documentation from the Docker build context to keep images lean and credential-safe.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full Tax Authority RAG stack: OpenSearch (Lucene k-NN), Redis Stack, Jaeger tracing, and the app container, all on a shared bridge network with health-gated startup order.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Two-stage Python 3.12-slim image: builder stage compiles native wheels, runtime stage installs them into a minimal non-root image with tini as PID 1 and pytest as the default entrypoint.

nodes.name: OpenSearch
nodes.summary: OpenSearch 2.18 single-node cluster with Lucene HNSW k-NN plugin, TLS security enabled, and 2 GB JVM heap — serves as the primary vector and keyword search store.

nodes.name: Redis Stack
nodes.summary: Redis Stack 7.4 instance providing semantic cache and session storage with a 1 GB LRU memory cap.

nodes.name: Jaeger
nodes.summary: Jaeger all-in-one tracing backend accepting OTLP gRPC on port 4317 with in-memory span storage (up to 10 000 traces) and a UI on port 16686.

nodes.name: App (pytest runner)
nodes.summary: Application container built from the project Dockerfile that runs the full RAG test suite via pytest, injecting AWS Bedrock credentials at runtime and writing JUnit XML reports to a mounted volume.

nodes.name: agentic-rag-designer agent
nodes.summary: Designs Module 3 (Agentic RAG & Self-Healing), specifying the LangGraph CRAG state machine with query transformation, retrieval grading, fallback policies, and citation verification to guarantee zero hallucination.

nodes.name: docker-runner agent
nodes.summary: Owns containerization and the test-execution loop — builds the Docker stack (app, OpenSearch, Redis, Jaeger), runs the evaluation test suite, debugs failures, and produces the test-results artifact consumed by report-compiler.

nodes.name: legal-domain-reviewer agent
nodes.summary: Reviews all module designs against Dutch tax law domain requirements (Belastingdienst, ECLI/ELI citations, FIOD classification, temporal tax-year validity), flagging gaps before the report-compiler finalises the document.

nodes.name: mlops-security-architect agent
nodes.summary: Designs Module 4 (Production Ops, Security & Evaluation), covering role-blind-safe semantic caching, RBAC pre-filtering via OpenSearch efficient_filter, CI/CD evaluation gates with Ragas/DeepEval, and OpenTelemetry observability.

nodes.name: process-report-author agent
nodes.summary: Produces the methodology/process report for non-engineering reviewers, narrating how all agents collaborated, their execution timeline, decision provenance, resource costs, and lessons learned from the multi-agent workflow.

nodes.name: rag-evaluation-engineer agent
nodes.summary: Builds the runnable pytest/Ragas/DeepEval test suite covering citation accuracy, RBAC red-teaming, temporal correctness, hybrid retrieval, semantic cache safety, latency budgets, and observability assertions.

nodes.name: rag-retrieval-architect agent
nodes.summary: Designs Modules 1 and 2 (Ingestion and Retrieval), specifying legal-corpus chunking, hierarchical metadata schema, OpenSearch HNSW configuration, Cohere multilingual embeddings/reranker, and hybrid BM25+dense fusion.

nodes.name: report-compiler agent
nodes.summary: Final assembly agent that resolves domain-review flags, enforces cross-module consistency, and compiles all module drafts plus test results into the single submission-ready FINAL-rag-architecture.md document.

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Excludes secrets, generated Jaeger traces, intermediate knowledge-graph artifacts, and the presentation folder from version control.

nodes.name: .mcp.json
nodes.summary: Registers the local AWS MCP server (stdio, npm-launched) so Claude Code tools can call AWS APIs directly from this project.

nodes.name: NEXT-SESSION-PROMPT
nodes.summary: A ready-to-paste session-bootstrap prompt that re-establishes the 6-phase multi-agent execution sequence, locked stack decisions, and guardrails in a fresh Claude Code session.

nodes.name: README
nodes.summary: Top-level project overview documenting the repository structure, core AWS/Cohere/CRAG stack, evaluation status, and recommended reading order for the Tax Authority Enterprise RAG system.

nodes.name: assignment
nodes.summary: Original four-module engineering assignment specifying the RAG system requirements: ingestion strategy, hybrid retrieval, agentic CRAG control loop, and production ops/security for a national tax authority.

nodes.name: launch-dashboard.ps1
nodes.summary: PowerShell script that sets the project root as GRAPH_DIR and starts the Vite-based knowledge graph dashboard from the understand-anything-plugin package.

nodes.name: Module 1 Ingestion (presentation)
nodes.summary: Interview study guide covering chunking strategy for Dutch legal text (structure-aware splitting, parent-document retrieval, metadata schema) and vector database selection for 20M+ chunks (OpenSearch HNSW with efficient_filter, FP16 quantization, RBAC-safe pre-filtering).

nodes.name: Module 2 Retrieval (presentation)
nodes.summary: Interview study guide covering hybrid search design (BM25 + dense k-NN, RRF fusion with adaptive regex router) and two-stage reranking (Cohere rerank-v3-5 on Bedrock, 100/100/60/8 Top-K cascade) for the Dutch fiscal corpus.

nodes.name: Module 3 Agentic RAG (presentation)
nodes.summary: Interview study guide covering query transformation routing (direct/decompose/HyDE/step-back), the LangGraph CRAG state machine with grader and exact fallback policy (Relevant/Ambiguous/Irrelevant), and citation verification for zero-hallucination guarantees.

nodes.name: Module 4 Ops & Security (presentation)
nodes.summary: Interview study guide covering semantic caching with role-bound cache keys and a 0.97/0.98 cosine threshold, database-level RBAC via OpenSearch efficient_filter inside HNSW traversal with three defense layers, and CI/CD evaluation gates (Ragas + DeepEval metrics, shadow/canary deployment, OTel/Jaeger observability).

nodes.name: Presentation Brief
nodes.summary: Planning brief for a 5-minute YouTube video presenting the Tax Authority RAG project to a non-technical audience, specifying a 10-slide structure covering the problem, multi-agent approach, security design, test results, and a Gamma slide deck plus companion script.

nodes.name: Presentation Answers
nodes.summary: Condensed one-stop reference with concise 60–90 second answers to all nine assignment interview questions across Modules 1–4, including key diagrams, exact configuration values, and fallback policy tables.

nodes.name: Slidev .gitignore
nodes.summary: Excludes node_modules, dist, remote assets, generated component type declarations, local environment files, and macOS metadata from version control for the Slidev presentation project.

nodes.name: Slidev Presentation README
nodes.summary: Developer guide for the Tax Authority RAG Slidev deck covering local dev setup, project structure (layouts, components, styles), editing conventions, speaker view shortcuts, PDF export, and rationale for using custom Vue components over Mermaid.

nodes.name: Slidev package.json
nodes.summary: NPM package manifest for the tax-rag-presentation Slidev project (v1.1.0), defining dev/build/export scripts and dependencies on @slidev/cli, @slidev/theme-default, Vue 3, and playwright-chromium for PDF export.

nodes.name: Enterprise RAG Slidev Presentation
nodes.summary: 1085-line Slidev deck covering four modules of the Tax Authority enterprise RAG system: Module 1 (hierarchical chunking, parent-child retrieval, vector DB selection, HNSW memory math), Module 2 (hybrid BM25+dense search, RRF fusion, cross-encoder reranking cascade), Module 3 (query transformation routing, CRAG state machine with grader and fallback policy, citation verifier), and Module 4 (role-bound semantic cache, mathematically leak-proof RBAC via OpenSearch efficient_filter, CI evaluation gates with Ragas/DeepEval, and OpenTelemetry observability).

nodes.name: FINAL RAG Architecture
nodes.summary: Consolidated submission-ready architecture covering all 4 modules: ingestion/retrieval, CRAG agentic loop, RBAC security, and production observability. Documents the five core design decisions: RBAC pre-filter inside HNSW traversal, split ELI/ECLI citation fields, full-depth citation anchor verification, role-bound year-scoped cache keys, and redaction-guarded refusal payloads.

nodes.name: Module 1-2: Ingestion & Retrieval
nodes.summary: Defines structure-aware chunking for Dutch fiscal law (256-token leaf / 1500-token parent), separate ELI/ECLI keyword fields, OpenSearch HNSW index schema, BM25+dense hybrid retrieval with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60), Cohere reranking to top-8, and parent-document fetch pattern.

nodes.name: Module 3: Agentic RAG & Self-Healing
nodes.summary: Specifies the LangGraph-based CRAG state machine with four query transform strategies (Direct, Decompose, HyDE, Step-back), deterministic routing by signal, Haiku 4.5 grader/generator/citation-judge nodes, retry loops with attempt caps, and structured refusal fallback. All nodes propagate RBAC role through state.

nodes.name: Module 4: Production Ops, Security & Evaluation
nodes.summary: Covers the four-principal threat model (helpdesk/inspector/legal/FIOD), role-bound SHA-256 semantic cache keys with 0.97 cosine floor in Redis Stack 7.4, RBAC pre-filter via OpenSearch efficient_filter, redaction_guard secondary gate, citation anchor verification at full lid/onderdeel/sub depth, OpenTelemetry observability, and Ragas/DeepEval evaluation harness.

nodes.name: CRAG (Corrective RAG)
nodes.summary: LangGraph state machine implementing a corrective retrieval loop: transform query (Direct | Decompose | HyDE | Step-back) → retrieve → grade documents (Relevant/Ambiguous/Irrelevant) → generate → verify citations, with attempt-capped retry and structured refusal fallback. RBAC role propagates through every node.

nodes.name: RBAC Filtering (efficient_filter in HNSW)
nodes.summary: Role-based access control enforced by OpenSearch's efficient_filter, which pre-computes a BitSet of allowed document IDs before HNSW graph traversal begins. FIOD chunks are invisible to the ANN walk, eliminating both content-leak and timing side-channel vectors. A secondary redaction_guard drops any post-RRF chunks exceeding the user's classification ceiling.

nodes.name: Hybrid Retrieval (BM25 + Dense + RRF)
nodes.summary: Parallel retrieval using BM25 (Dutch analyzer, top-100) and Cohere dense embeddings (embed-multilingual-v3, 1024-dim, top-100) fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60) to produce a post-RRF top-60 candidate set, then reranked by Cohere rerank-v3-5:0 to top-8 for generation context.

nodes.name: Citation Verification (Zero-Hallucination Grounding)
nodes.summary: Anchor regex + NLI grounding judge operating at full (doc_id, article, lid, onderdeel, sub) hierarchy depth for ELI legislation and ECLI case-law citations. A citation correct at the article level but wrong at lid/onderdeel is treated as a legal hallucination. Unsupported citations trigger a generation retry; cap hit produces a structured refusal filtered through redaction_guard.

nodes.name: .env
nodes.summary: Active runtime environment variables supplying AWS credentials, Bedrock model IDs, OpenSearch connection details, Redis URL, and Jaeger OTLP endpoint for the Tax Authority RAG stack.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Annotated template listing all required environment variables with placeholder values and inline guidance on AWS credentials, Bedrock model IDs, OpenSearch, Redis, and observability settings.

nodes.name: IAM-POLICY.md
nodes.summary: Documents the least-privilege IAM policy required by the app container, covering bedrock:InvokeModel permissions for Cohere Embed, Cohere Rerank, and Claude Haiku 4.5 cross-region profile, plus sts:GetCallerIdentity for startup validation.

nodes.name: compose.override.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose development override that exposes host ports for OpenSearch, Redis Stack, Jaeger, and the app container, and bind-mounts source directories for hot-reload and interactive debugging.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Pinned Python dependencies for the app container, including boto3, opensearch-py, redis, ragas, deepeval, pytest plugins, and a version-aligned set of OpenTelemetry packages constrained by deepeval.

nodes.name: app healthcheck
nodes.summary: Shell startup probe that calls AWS STS GetCallerIdentity via boto3 to verify AWS credentials are accessible inside the app container. Exits 0 on success, 1 on failure.

nodes.name: jaeger healthcheck
nodes.summary: Shell healthcheck that probes the Jaeger all-in-one admin HTTP endpoint on port 14269 and expects a 200 response.

nodes.name: opensearch healthcheck
nodes.summary: Shell healthcheck that polls the OpenSearch cluster health API waiting for at least yellow status, handling the demo-cert first-boot race condition. Credentials and URL are configurable via environment variables.

nodes.name: redis healthcheck
nodes.summary: Shell healthcheck that sends a PING command via redis-cli and asserts a PONG response. Port is configurable via the REDIS_PORT environment variable.

nodes.name: Master Plan
nodes.summary: Top-level project plan for the Tax Authority Enterprise RAG system, consolidating the locked AWS Bedrock + OpenSearch + Jaeger stack, execution phases, agent sequencing, promotion gates, and open risks.

nodes.name: Agentic RAG Designer Plan
nodes.summary: Planning document for Module 3 covering the CRAG state machine, query transformation routing, retrieval grader design, citation verification, and loop guard logic using Claude Haiku 4.5 on Bedrock.

nodes.name: Docker Runner Plan
nodes.summary: Planning document for the docker-runner agent, detailing the multi-stage Dockerfile, docker-compose service stack (OpenSearch, Redis Stack, Jaeger, MinIO (scoped out)), eval orchestration scripts, and anticipated failure mitigations.

nodes.name: MLOps Security Architect Plan
nodes.summary: Planning document for Module 4 covering semantic cache design, three-layer RBAC enforcement, CI/CD promotion gates, and OpenTelemetry observability via Jaeger for the production RAG system.

nodes.name: RAG Evaluation Engineer Plan
nodes.summary: Planning document for the test suite covering ten pytest files with Ragas and DeepEval metrics, a 50-pair golden set, adversarial RBAC red-team tests, latency benchmarks, and a synthetic fixture corpus.

nodes.name: RAG Retrieval Architect Plan
nodes.summary: Planning document for Modules 1 and 2 covering structure-aware chunking, hierarchical metadata schema, Amazon OpenSearch HNSW configuration, hybrid BM25 + dense fusion, Cohere reranking, and the top-K cascade latency budget.

nodes.name: Process Report
nodes.summary: Governance and methodology report describing the seven-agent pipeline that produced the Tax Authority RAG architecture across six sequential phases over two calendar days.

nodes.name: Agent Timings
nodes.summary: JSONL log of per-agent execution metrics including phase, start/end timestamps, duration, token counts, tool uses, and completion status for all nine agent runs.

nodes.name: Jaeger Observability Report
nodes.summary: Distributed tracing analysis of 16 representative traces across 3 query scenarios captured via Jaeger, documenting pipeline latency breakdown and RBAC span propagation.

nodes.name: JUnit XML Results
nodes.summary: Machine-readable pytest JUnit XML output recording 179 passing test cases (0 errors, 0 failures) across citation, RBAC, retrieval, latency, cache, and temporal correctness test suites.

nodes.name: Test Results
nodes.summary: Human-readable test execution report documenting the Docker stack (OpenSearch, Redis, Jaeger), pytest run outcomes, and the 170 mock plus 3 live Redis integration passes.

nodes.name: Understand Session Report
nodes.summary: Session report from the /understand skill run on this project, describing the multi-phase knowledge-graph build and project statistics for the Tax Authority Enterprise RAG codebase.

nodes.name: YouTube Script
nodes.summary: Five-minute conversational video script for a LinkedIn/YouTube audience explaining how an AI multi-agent pipeline designed a secure enterprise RAG system for a national tax authority interview assignment.

nodes.name: trace-cache-hit-raw.json
nodes.summary: Jaeger trace export showing a cache hit scenario in the RAG pipeline.

nodes.name: trace-generation-raw.json
nodes.summary: Jaeger trace export capturing a full generation flow through the RAG pipeline.

nodes.name: trace-refusal-raw.json
nodes.summary: Jaeger trace export recording a refusal scenario in the RAG pipeline.

nodes.name: debug-shell.sh
nodes.summary: Execs an interactive bash shell (or an arbitrary command) inside the running tax-rag-app container, starting the Docker Compose stack first if the container is not already up.

nodes.name: run-eval.sh
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full evaluation pipeline — build, start, health-wait, seed corpus, run pytest, parse JUnit XML results, enforce the RBAC threshold gate, write a test-results.md report, and tear down the stack.

nodes.name: seed-corpus.sh
nodes.summary: Synthesizes ~250 role-tagged tax-law fixture documents and pushes them into the OpenSearch 'tax-docs-test' index by invoking the Python seeder inside the app container (or locally as a fallback).

nodes.name: Claude MCP Settings
nodes.summary: Project-level Claude Code settings enabling the AWS MCP server for Bedrock and STS operations.

nodes.name: Claude Local Permissions
nodes.summary: Local Claude Code permission allowlist covering AWS CLI, Docker, pytest, PowerShell, and GitHub CLI commands accumulated during project development.

nodes.name: Slidev Components & Layouts
nodes.summary: Vue 3 components for the Slidev presentation: architecture diagrams (AgentGraph, PipeFlow, LayerStack), timeline charts (AgentGantt, Waterfall), and layout templates.

nodes.name: CI Promotion Gate (assert_thresholds)
nodes.summary: Parses JUnit XML from the test run and enforces hard CI promotion gates: RBAC Leak Rate = 0.00, Citation Accuracy = 1.00, Faithfulness >= 0.95, ContextPrecision >= 0.85, ContextRecall >= 0.90. Exits non-zero if any hard gate fails.

nodes.name: Knowledge Graph Cleanup
nodes.summary: Normalises the .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json in-place: flattens non-standard node fields, remaps invalid edge types to spec-valid equivalents, removes orphan concept nodes, and drops dangling edges. Writes a backup before overwriting.

nodes.name: Demo Trace Emitter
nodes.summary: Emits five representative RAG pipeline traces to Jaeger via OTLP (gRPC) using realistic latency numbers from Module 4 §4.2. Covers cache-hit, RBAC-blocked, refusal, and normal retrieval scenarios.

nodes.name: Golden Q&A Dataset
nodes.summary: 59 curated question-answer pairs spanning Dutch tax legislation, case law, policy, and e-learning documents across 2020-2024, including RBAC-must-refuse, temporal, ECLI exact-match, and ambiguous/refusal cases. Used as the parameterised source for all evaluation test modules.

nodes.name: pytest Configuration
nodes.summary: Configures pytest test discovery, async mode, and custom markers (slow, redteam, latency, integration, citation, cache, temporal, refusal, observability). Default addopts exclude slow tests for the fast loop.

nodes.name: Test Plan
nodes.summary: Documents the full evaluation suite: per-file test counts, threat/quality dimensions, pass criteria, domain-review coverage mapping, promotion gate constants, and docker-runner hand-off procedure for the ~75 test functions covering ~250 effective assertions.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README and the original assignment brief to understand what was built: a secure enterprise RAG system for a national tax authority with zero-hallucination citations and RBAC-enforced document filtering.

tour.title: Architecture — Ingestion & Hybrid Retrieval
tour.description: The retrieval layer uses BM25 + dense embedding fusion with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, HNSW settings (m=32, ef=256), and Cohere reranking. Explore module 1-2 design and the hybrid-retrieval concept.

tour.title: Architecture — CRAG Agentic Loop
tour.description: Module 3 implements a LangGraph state machine: transform query → retrieve → grade (Haiku judge) → generate → verify citations. The CRAG concept node shows how all pieces connect.

tour.title: Architecture — RBAC & Observability
tour.description: RBAC uses efficient_filter inside HNSW traversal to prevent timing side-channel leaks. Role-bound Redis cache keys, zero-hallucination citation guard, and OpenTelemetry → Jaeger tracing.

tour.title: Consolidated Architecture Document
tour.description: The FINAL-rag-architecture.md is the submission-ready document that integrates all four modules into a cohesive design with cross-cutting concerns like RBAC, observability, and CI gates.

tour.title: Multi-Agent Build Pipeline
tour.description: This project was itself built by a 7-agent Claude pipeline. The Master Plan coordinated agents for retrieval design, CRAG, ops/security, evaluation, Docker, legal review, and final compilation. The Process Report documents methodology and agent timings.

tour.title: Test Fixtures & Evaluation Harness
tour.description: conftest.py (1207 lines) defines the evaluation foundation: synthetic MOCK_CORPUS with 250 tax-law chunks, ChunkMeta, RAGResponse, UserContext, MockRAGClient, and HaikuJudge for live integration tests.

tour.title: RBAC Red-Team Tests
tour.description: test_rbac_redteam.py (637 lines) covers 7 threat classes: direct FIOD query, semantic proximity attacks, cache poisoning, prompt injection, existence disclosure, role-ceiling violations, and audit counters.

tour.title: Citation & Retrieval Quality Tests
tour.description: Citation accuracy test enforces a hard 1.00 gate — no partial credit. Hybrid retrieval tests validate nDCG@5 improvement from reranking. Temporal correctness tests validate tax-year versioning.

tour.title: Docker Infrastructure Stack
tour.description: docker-compose.yml orchestrates OpenSearch, Redis Stack, Jaeger, and the app container. Health-gated startup, TLS-secured OpenSearch, role-based Redis, and OTLP trace export.

tour.title: CI Evaluation Pipeline
tour.description: run-eval.sh is the full CI pipeline: build → up → seed → pytest → JUnit parse → RBAC hard-fail gate → report → down. assert_thresholds.py enforces Citation=1.00 and RBAC Leak=0.00.
